protocol.response_format_note: Use structured_text for Qwen-family backends until direct JSON prompting is revalidated.

allowed_claims: pilot-only Gemma4/Qwen stress diagnostics if model outputs are generated under this manifest
stress candidate selection and target-visible visual QA provenance
forbidden_claims: not a final GRScenes benchmark result
not evidence that no-MDL conversion preserves VLM grounding in general

visual_review.visible_evidence: A blue hanging bag-like object is visible below a hat in the central wall area in both images.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Target reads more like a handbag or purse than a clear backpack; retake from an angle showing straps, back panel, or fuller body shape.

visual_review.visible_evidence: A blue hanging bag is centered and visible in both images with comparable camera/view.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Object reads more like a small handbag or satchel than a clearly identifiable backpack; retake lower or closer to show backpack-defining features.

visual_review.visible_evidence: A small blue hanging bag/backpack-like object is centered on the wall and visible in both images.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Object reads more like a handbag or purse than a clearly identifiable backpack; retake front-facing with backpack-specific features visible.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Two framed wall pictures with black-and-white horse/rider artwork are clearly visible in both images.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Grounding target is ambiguous because two picture instances are visible; retake with the intended picture centered and dominant.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Wall-mounted framed pictures are clearly visible; the main image appears to show a horse/rider drawing or print.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Right picture is cropped and left foreground structure distracts; retake so the full intended framed picture is visible.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Faucet is visible near lower center in both images, with spout/handle shape identifiable.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Strong material/lighting differences dominate the before/after view; usable for target grounding but not clean visual-fidelity evidence.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Faucet is clearly visible and identifiable in both images, centered near the sink/counter with the same close camera view.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Camera is comparable, but red-on-red original materials versus black/white converted materials make the visual-domain shift too large for clean before/after preservation.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Faucet is clearly visible in both images; vertical body, horizontal spout, and handle/lever are identifiable from the same close-up view.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Strong red-vs-black/white material mismatch dominates the before/after view; useful stress evidence but not clean preservation evidence.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Faucet is clearly visible in both images; upright body, spout, and handle are identifiable with close matched framing.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Converted view has major red-to-black/white material/color shift, weakening visual-preservation claims while remaining useful for material-shift stress testing.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Wall clock is centered, large, and identifiable in both images; circular frame, hands, and radial/axis marks are visible.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Target is excellent for grounding, but severe material/texture/color shift prevents treating this as clean visual-preservation PASS.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Wall/table clock is visible and identifiable in both images; circular frame, hub, radial spokes, and hands are readable.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Camera angle is comparable, but nearby wall/door geometry and ceiling overhang crowd the object; dark converted material and glossy red original material reduce clean visual preservation confidence.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Wall clock is clearly visible on the central wall or pillar in both images; circular rim, hands, and center hub are identifiable with strongly matched viewpoint.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Fine clock details are thin and slightly soft, and the bright area on the right is blown out, but these issues do not hide the clock.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Clock is centered, larger, and more front-facing in both images; rim, hands, and wall placement are clear, and A/B framing is highly comparable.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Scene appearance changes substantially under the high-contrast material condition, so the pair is best treated as grounding stress evidence rather than clean material-preservation evidence.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Pillow is centered and identifiable in both images; rectangular cushion shape, dark border, white band, stripes, and button-like details are visible.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Framing is matched, but saturated red glare in the original and darker shadowed converted view create a strong lighting/material-context shift that can confound fair before/after grounding comparison.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Bottle is centered, upright, and identifiable in both A and B; surrounding objects provide scale/context.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Conservative adjudication: one reviewer flagged substantial material/color shift in B, while another treated it as minor. Usable for material-shift stress, not for material-fidelity preservation.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Bottle is visible in both images, but B has a very dark body that blends into shadow/table.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Retake with brighter fill or less dark material contrast for clearer VLM grounding.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Plate stack is centered and identifiable in both A and B.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: A/B have strong material and lighting differences; B background is very dark. Usable, but comparison caveat.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Framed wall pictures are visible in both images from similar angle.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Dense grid overlay and oblique framing reduce content clarity; retake closer/front-on if picture details matter.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Bottle is visible in both images, especially B; A shows it but red/dark material reduces separation.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: A/B material and lighting differ substantially, and A bottle blends into the red/dark background. Usable with caveat.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Round wall clock with rim and hands is clearly visible in both A and B; framing is comparable.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Minor caveat: dark surrounding areas, but target remains identifiable.

visual_review.visible_evidence: A stemmed cocktail/cup-like glass is centered and identifiable in both A and B; contents/props visible.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: A/B material/color differences are strong, and the object reads more like a cocktail glass than a generic cup; usable with category caveat.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Stemmed cup/glass is centered and identifiable in both A and B; straw and cherries help match geometry.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Low contrast: A red cup on red table, B black cup on dark table loses bowl/detail. Usable but segmentation/grounding may be harder.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Target remains centered, full height visible, and comparable across A/B.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: B cup body is very dark and blends into the background/table; surface detail is mostly lost, though silhouette is clear.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Cup/glass is visible in both images with consistent camera angle and full stem/base mostly in frame.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Overhead clutter and side object add distraction; B again has poor dark-material contrast. Still usable for VLM grounding.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Cup/glass is identifiable and similarly framed in A/B; no target truncation.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Background object is close behind the straw/rim, and B has dark cup/table contrast loss; comparison remains mostly fair.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Cup is centered and identifiable in both A and B; rim and cylindrical body visible. A has low red-on-red contrast; B has strong black/white material streaking.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Usable for grounding, but material/table differences and B texture artifacts reduce clean A/B comparability. Retake on neutral surface if material fidelity matters.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Cup is clearly visible in both views with handle and rim apparent. Framing is close and useful.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: B shows smeared/blotchy black-white material and A/B surface colors differ; comparison is usable but visually uneven.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Cup body and rim are visible in both; handle is partly hidden at the far side but still inferable.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: A has glare and low red-on-red separation; B handle is mostly obscured and material appears vertically streaked. Retake with a clearer side angle if handle identity is important.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Cup is large, centered, identifiable, and the handle/rim/body are visible in both A and B. Framing is useful and views are comparable.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Minor material differences and B texture streaking, but not enough to block grounding or pair comparison.

visual_review.visible_evidence: Both A and B show a mug/cup with handle in close, centered framing.
visual_review.risk_or_retake: Material/color differs strongly between A and B; B has smeared/patchy surface artifacts. Usable, but caveated.
